pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to pydomain: what it is, why it exists, the five adoption levels, and the layered module architecture. Links to all major child sections.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Installation, requirements (Python 3.12+, Pydantic v2, uuid-utils), verification, dev-extras, and a quickstart walkthrough showing the five adoption levels in practice.

pages.title: Architecture and Module Boundaries
pages.purpose: Explains the strict layered dependency graph (ddd → cqrs/es → infrastructure → testing), the dependency rules enforced by pytest-archon, and how each module maps to an adoption level.

pages.title: Domain Layer (pydomain.ddd)
pages.purpose: Overview of the tactical DDD primitives in pydomain.ddd — the innermost layer with zero infrastructure dependencies. Links to child pages for each major concept.

pages.title: Entities, Value Objects, and Aggregate Roots
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for Entity[TId], ValueObject, and AggregateRoot[TId]: Pydantic v2 base classes, identity semantics, immutability, the _pending_events buffer, pull_events(), and optimistic concurrency via the version field.

pages.title: Domain Events
pages.purpose: Covers DomainEvent: frozen Pydantic model, event_id/occurred_at/event_version fields, the stamp() method for injecting correlation_id and causation_id without mutating the frozen object, and the publish-after-commit pattern.

pages.title: Repository, Factory, and Domain Services
pages.purpose: Explains the Repository[T, TId] protocol (save/get_by_id/delete/pull_events), Factory[T] vs ReconstitutionFactory[T] distinction, DomainService marker class, Specification pattern with composition (and_/or_/not_), and IdGenerator/Uuid7Generator.

pages.title: CQRS Layer (pydomain.cqrs)
pages.purpose: Overview of the CQRS module: commands, queries, buses, pipeline behaviors, unit of work, projections, and integration events. Links to child pages for each subsystem.

pages.title: Commands, Queries, and Buses
pages.purpose: Covers Command[TResult] and Query[TResult] frozen models, CommandResult/QueryResult/EmptyCommandResult, CommandBus and QueryBus registration and dispatch, handler protocols (CommandHandler, QueryHandler, EventHandler), and the fail-loud vs fail-independent semantics.

pages.title: Pipeline Behaviors and Middleware
pages.purpose: Explains the MessagePipeline onion pattern, PipelineBehavior protocol, MessageContext carrier, MessageKind enum, and the four built-in behaviors: LoggingBehavior, ValidationBehavior, IdempotencyBehavior (with ProcessedCommandStore/MISSING sentinel), and AggregateLockingBehavior (with LockProvider/LockKeyResolver/deadlock prevention).

pages.title: Unit of Work
pages.purpose: Covers the UnitOfWork protocol and AbstractUnitOfWork ABC: async context manager lifecycle, commit/rollback, event stamping with correlation/causation IDs, collect_events(), and the _on_post_commit hook for outbox patterns.

pages.title: Projections and Integration Events
pages.purpose: Covers the CQRS Projection[StateT] protocol (left-fold pattern, apply/rebuild), ProjectionStore protocol, and IntegrationEvent (primitives-only constraint, string event_id/occurred_at for broker serialization).

pages.title: Saga Subsystem (pydomain.cqrs.saga)
pages.purpose: Overview of the saga orchestration subsystem for long-running business processes: the on() DSL, SagaState aggregate, SagaManager lifecycle, and compensation. Links to child pages for state management and advanced features.

pages.title: Saga Definition and Lifecycle
pages.purpose: Explains Saga[S] base class, the on() DSL for event-to-command mapping with optional compensation, SagaStatus state machine (PENDING→RUNNING→SUSPENDED→COMPLETED/FAILED→COMPENSATING→COMPENSATED), SagaManager load-handle-save-dispatch cycle, SagaRegistry auto-binding, and SagaRepository protocol.

pages.title: Saga State, Compensation, and Advanced Features
pages.purpose: Deep dive into SagaState fields (step_history, processed_event_ids, compensation_stack, pending_commands, suspension/timeout), LIFO compensation execution, idempotency via processed_event_ids, suspension/resume/timeout patterns, crash recovery, pruning policies (StepThresholdPruningPolicy), and memory bounds.

pages.title: Event Sourcing Layer (pydomain.es)
pages.purpose: Overview of the event sourcing module: EventSourcedAggregateRoot, EventStore, EventStream, EventSourcedRepository, snapshots, upcasting, and projections. Links to child pages for each major subsystem.

pages.title: EventSourcedAggregateRoot and EventStore
pages.purpose: Covers EventSourcedAggregateRoot[TId]: _apply/_when/_replay methods, dual-mode event application, version tracking, _snapshot_schema_version. Also covers EventStore protocol (append_to_stream with optimistic concurrency, read_stream, read_all), EventStream frozen value object, and DuplicateCommandError idempotency.

pages.title: Snapshots and Schema Versioning
pages.purpose: Explains the Snapshot value object, SnapshotStore protocol, SnapshotPolicy/SnapshotThresholdPolicy, SnapshotSchemaPolicy/RejectStaleSnapshotPolicy, StaleSnapshotError, and the snapshot-first hydration strategy in EventSourcedRepository. Covers _snapshot_schema_version and schema evolution.

pages.title: Event Upcasting and Schema Migration
pages.purpose: Covers EventUpcaster base class (source_type, source_version, target_version, _transform), UpcasterRegistry chain resolution and cycle detection, UpcastError, and how EventRegistry integrates upcasting during deserialization. Includes event_version field on DomainEvent.

pages.title: Event-Sourced Projections and Subscriptions
pages.purpose: Covers EventSourcedProjection ABC (convention-based _when_* dispatch, checkpoint tracking, name/version class vars), CheckpointStore protocol, Subscription dataclass, and SubscriptionRunner polling loop with at-least-once delivery semantics and failure backoff.

pages.title: Infrastructure Layer (pydomain.infrastructure)
pages.purpose: Overview of the infrastructure wiring layer: MessageBus facade, bootstrap composition root, EventRegistry, MessageBroker, and InboundEventGateway. Links to child pages for each component.

pages.title: MessageBus and Application Bootstrap
pages.purpose: Covers the MessageBus Level-3 facade (unified dispatch for Command/Query/DomainEvent, register_command/register_query/register_event, call-local event queue, failure isolation for event handlers, sequential event processing), the bootstrap() composition root function, and the Application class lifecycle (dispatch/shutdown).

pages.title: EventRegistry, MessageBroker, and InboundEventGateway
pages.purpose: Covers EventRegistry (register/serialize/deserialize, GenericDomainEvent fallback, UpcasterRegistry integration), MessageBroker protocol (publish/start/stop), InMemoryMessageBroker test double, InboundEventGateway Anti-Corruption Layer (register_translation, MessageSubscriber protocol, poison-message handling), and the inbound message processing pipeline.

pages.title: Testing Infrastructure (pydomain.testing)
pages.purpose: Overview of the pydomain.testing module: the complete set of in-memory fakes and test doubles that allow testing domain logic without any real infrastructure. Links to child pages for DDD/CQRS fakes and ES fakes.

pages.title: DDD and CQRS Test Doubles
pages.purpose: Covers FakeRepository (in-memory aggregate store with pull_events), FakeUnitOfWork (AbstractUnitOfWork subclass for tests), FakeLockProvider (asyncio.Lock-based), FakeProcessedCommandStore (idempotency testing), FakeSagaRepository, and InMemoryProjectionStore. Explains how to wire these into test suites.

pages.title: Event Sourcing Test Doubles
pages.purpose: Covers FakeEventStore (in-memory EventStore with ConcurrencyError/DuplicateCommandError logic, global log), FakeSnapshotStore, FakeCheckpointStore, InMemoryMessageBroker, and InMemoryMessageSubscriber. Explains how to use these for SubscriptionRunner and EventSourcedRepository tests.

pages.title: Developer Tooling and Contribution
pages.purpose: Overview of the development environment, CI/CD workflows, commit conventions, and the VoltAgent-driven development workflow. Links to child pages for tooling and workflow.

pages.title: Code Quality and CI/CD
pages.purpose: Covers the pre-commit pipeline (ruff linting/formatting, mypy type checking, commit message validation via check-commit-msg.sh), GitHub Actions workflows (test, lint-commits, block-breaking, publish), CODEOWNERS, and the pyproject.toml configuration.

pages.title: Architecture Decision Records
pages.purpose: Index and summary of all 59 ADRs documenting the key design decisions in pydomain, grouped by layer. Covers decisions on protocols vs ABCs, Pydantic v2, async-only API, exception hierarchy, identity semantics, CQRS patterns, saga design, event sourcing, and infrastructure wiring.
